items.headline: Giants call up top prospect Bryce Eldridge to try to jolt a sputtering offense
items.description:
SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The San Francisco Giants called up top prospect Bryce Eldridge on Monday to help boost their sputtering offense.
Eldridge was the designated hitter batting in the No. 7 slot in manager Tony Vitello’s lineup for Monday night’s game against the San Diego Padres.

items.headline: Edwards cleared to play for Timberwolves in series opener against Spurs after expedited rehab
items.description:
SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Minnesota Timberwolves All-Star Anthony Edwards has been cleared to play Monday night against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1 of the Western Conference semifinals, returning 10 days after hyperextending his left knee.
Edwards was expected to miss at least the first two games of the series in San Antonio, but the 6-foot-4 guard expedited his rehabilitation to play in the opener.

items.headline: Astros scratch catcher Yainer Diaz with a left abdominal injury before start of Dodgers series
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Catcher Yainer Diaz was scratched from Houston’s lineup about an hour before the start of a series against the Los Angeles Dodgers on Monday night because of a left abdominal injury.
Christian Vázquez took his spot in the lineup and was batting ninth.

items.headline: Demoted to minors, Anthony Volpe has to earn back New York Yankees shortstop job from José Caballero
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Anthony Volpe has to earn back his New York Yankees job, just like any other prospect.
New York optioned the Gold Glove-winning shortstop to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre on Sunday following the end of a 20-day minor league injury rehabilitation assignment that followed Oct. 14 surgery to repair a torn labrum in his left shoulder.

items.headline: Chicago Sky waive Hailey Van Lith and sign Natasha Cloud
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago Sky waived point guard Hailey Van Lith after drafting her in the first round last year and signed guard Natasha Cloud on Monday.
Van Lith struggled after being drafted with the No. 11 overall pick. She averaged 3.5 points in 29 games following a standout career at Louisville, LSU and TCU.

items.headline: Tippett, Nikishin both out again for Game 2 of Flyers-Hurricanes 2nd-round series in NHL playoffs
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Philadelphia Flyers forward Owen Tippett and Carolina Hurricanes defenseman Alexander Nikishin will miss Monday's Game 2 of the second-round series in the NHL playoffs.
Both players sat out Carolina's 3-0 win in Game 1.

items.headline: Wild seek a reset after a crazy 9-6 to Colorado in Game 1, coach John Hynes ponders lineup
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — Kirill Kaprizov arrived in the banquet room Monday for media availability wearing a pair of hotel slippers.
The Minnesota forward's attitude about the series, even after a wild 9-6 loss to Colorado in Game 1, proved just as chill as his footwear choice.

items.headline: Marlins promote catcher Mack from Triple-A, demote struggling Ramírez to minors
items.description:
MIAMI (AP) — The Miami Marlins promoted highly-rated catcher Joe Mack from Triple-A and demoted slumping catcher Agustín Ramírez before the club’s game against the Philadelphia Phillies on Monday.
Mack, rated the fifth catching prospect in the major leagues and 54th fourth overall, is hitting .244 with three homers and nine RBIs through 24 games with the Marlins’ affiliate in Jacksonville.

items.headline: Mariners catcher Cal Raleigh sitting out a 3rd straight game due to a sore right side
items.description:
SEATTLE (AP) — Seattle Mariners catcher Cal Raleigh is out of the starting lineup for the third straight game with a sore right side, manager Dan Wilson said Monday.
Raleigh began experiencing soreness following Friday night’s game against the Kansas City Royals and was a late scratch on Saturday.

items.headline: Auriemma says he felt 'dumb' for the way exchange with Staley played out following Final Four game
items.description:
STORRS, Conn. (AP) — Six weeks after his team lost to South Carolina in the Final Four, UConn coach Geno Auriemma said Monday that he felt “dumb” for how his heated postgame exchange with Gamecocks coach Dawn Staley played out in front of a national audience.
“When I walked into the locker room afterward with the coaches, you are just shaking your head, thinking five more seconds, you couldn’t keep it in for five more seconds,” Auriemma said in his first news conference since then.
